symbols: spiked wheel (Catherine wheel) — the instrument of intended torture that miraculously shattered, symbol of divine intervention for the faithful
crown or diadem — Catherine's royal birth and her spiritual sovereignty as bride of Christ
gold ground — the heavenly realm, divine glory, and the transcendence of sacred space
halo with radiating spikes — sanctity and the crown of martyrdom, the light of holiness
palm branches (held) — victory over death through martyrdom, the triumph of faith over persecution
description: A bust-length portrait of a young woman set against a luminous gold ground. She wears a dark red garment and a blue headband or crown adorned with a jewel. A large radiant halo with pointed rays surrounds her head. She holds several dark arrow-like shafts or spikes in her right hand -- attributes associated with the spiked wheel. Her face is rendered in the soft, rosy style of early fifteenth-century Portuguese-Italian panel painting, with delicate features and a serene expression. The panel shows visible age cracking throughout the paint surface.

scripture_refs.reason: The exhortation to be faithful unto death to receive the crown of life, embodied in Catherine's steadfast martyrdom.
Christ's promise that the Spirit will speak through his followers when brought before authorities, fulfilled in Catherine's legendary debate with the philosophers.
The crown of righteousness laid up for those who finish the race and keep the faith, reflected in Catherine's crown and palm.
